name: Fallout 76
description: Bethesda Game Studios welcome you to Fallout 76. Twenty-five years after the bombs fall, you and your fellow Vault Dwellers emerge into post-nuclear America. Explore a vast wasteland in this open-world multiplayer addition to the Fallout story.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system
  • OS *: Windows 8.1/10 (64-bit versions)
  • Processor: Intel Core i5-6600k 3.5 GHz /AMD Ryzen 3 1300X 3.5 GHz or equivalent
  • Memory: 8 GB RAM
  • Graphics: NVIDIA GTX 780 3GB /AMD Radeon R9 285 2GB or equivalent
  • Network: Broadband Internet connection
  • Storage: 96 GB available space
comment1:
I've been playing since the Wastelanders update and have put in a good 5k hours into this game, and honestly, I couldn't recommend it. 

The latest updates have been nothing but half-baked, buggy trash, the devs keep introducing more and more greedy ways to monetize the game. They'll let gameplay-breaking bugs sit for actual years without fixing them, Atom Shop items are overpriced and more often than not broken on release and will take months, if not years, to be fixed despite some items costing upwards of 20 dollars, and the devs will rarely listen to community feedback and often push updates that break fundamental aspects of the game despite being warned about said issues before the updates are brought to the live game in the pts sever.

despite all the time in invested in this game i could not in good faith recommend it to anyone who actually likes fallout 

this game embodies everything wrong with the current direction bethesda is heading in and if it keeps going in the direction it is its only going to get worse
comment2: WAY better than the launch. Played this game during launch on console and genuinely felt like finding Todd in his home and forcing him to play that pile of straight garbage as a form of punishment. It's chillin now though. Lots of fun little dorky things to do and it honestly feels like the PVE aspect of Destiny 2 without the absolute goblins and mouth breathers watching you from the chair in the corner of their bedroom, backseat gaming you the whole time. And getting builds is going is fairly easy. The late game portion takes a good bit of time but it's not like you can't have fun before that. The people are nice. Met a handful of characters but they're all still chill and kind folk. The way the community handles new players is great. They look out for them and help them get going without much in compensation. I will say one gripe I have is how god awful their quality of life changes are. Storage is basically "pay to win" and it's nearly impossible to get to end game without personally handing your cash to Todd and letting him unload on your face. Other than that, fairly decent.
comment3: I poured money into this game thinking oh it'll take a day to get fallout 1st started. nah we are now at day 5 and Bethesduh sits their fingering themselves not giving a ♥♥♥♥ to fix their game or give a fk about what the players desire. To Todd Coward, you have taken a beloved franchise and ringed it out for as much money as you could. Thanks for fking your player base. Fk this game fk Tod Coward and Fk the ♥♥♥♥ support team not doing a dayum thing.
